title: Mary Ruane
content:
The sky-like girl whom we knew!
            She dressed herself to go to the fair
        In a dress of white and blue;
        A white lace cap, and ribbons white
            She wore in her hair;
        She does not hear in the night
        Her mother crying for her,
            Where,
                Deep down in the sea,
        She rolls and lingers to and fro
                Unweariedly.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Stephens, titled "Mary Ruane", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
